title: Ancillary Data
description: Data acquired on the same platform but not obtained from the main Sensor itself (usually provided as part of Level 0 Data), with the primary purpose of serving the processing of main instrument Data.

definitions.definition: Data acquired on the same platform but not obtained from the main Sensor itself (usually provided as part of Level 0 Data), with the primary purpose of serving the processing of main instrument Data (e.g. Position and velocity of platform).
definitions.notes: This can be divided into data referred to as spacecraft 'engineering', 'core housekeeping' or 'subsystem' data obtained from other parts of the platform, and includes parameters such as orbit position and velocity, attitude and its range of change, time, temperatures, pressures, jet firings, water dumps, internally produced magnetic fields, and other environmental measurements. Ancillary refers to data that exist purely to serve the data processing.
definitions.sources:
- ESA/CEOS?, modified
- EO Data Stewardship Glossary

___

definitions.definition: Data other than instrument measurements, originating in the instrument itself or from the satellite, required to perform processing of the Data. Includes orbit Data, attitude Data, time Information, and spacecraft engineering Data, Calibration Data, Data quality Information, and Data from other instruments or earth system models.
